message:
fix(sync): mutex baton-passing coverage and OnceCell::set() retry on cancelled initializer

mutex: Add two edge-case tests for the lock handoff protocol:

  drop_woken_future_passes_baton: Verifies that when unlock() wakes
  waiter A but A's LockFuture is dropped without being polled (e.g.
  select! cancellation), the Drop impl detects the lock is free and
  passes the baton to waiter B. Without this baton-passing, B would
  sleep indefinitely despite the lock being available.

  try_lock_steal_does_not_lose_waiter: Verifies that when a try_lock()
  steals the lock before the woken waiter can poll, the waiter correctly
  re-registers at the front and acquires the lock once the stealer
  releases it, rather than being permanently lost.

once_cell: Fix OnceCell::set() to handle concurrent initialization
correctly. Previously, set() treated both INITIALIZED and INITIALIZING
states the same -- returning Err(value) immediately. This was wrong
for INITIALIZING: if another task is currently initializing the cell
and that initialization is cancelled, set() should retry rather than
give up. Now set() loops: on INITIALIZING it calls
wait_for_init_blocking() then re-checks state. If init completed,
returns Err. If init was cancelled (state reset to UNINIT), retries
the CAS. Adds two tests:

  set_waits_for_inflight_initializer_and_returns_err_when_initialized:
  Spawns an initializer behind a barrier, calls set() while init is
  in-flight, verifies set() blocks and returns Err(9) after the
  initializer completes with value 7.

  set_retries_after_cancelled_inflight_initializer: Starts an async
  init that returns Pending forever, spawns set(42) which blocks on
  INITIALIZING, drops the init future (cancelling it), and verifies
  set(42) retries and succeeds with Ok(()).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
